title: Homer And Neds Hail Mary Pass

jokes.text: Aren't you that crazy Cat Lady? Yes, I am. But thanks to this psychoactive medication, I enjoy brief moments of lucidity. Those are just Reese's Pieces.
jokes.setup: Cat Lady claims medication gives her lucidity
jokes.punchline: It's just candy
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdism with perfect character integration - only Cat Lady would mistake candy for medication while maintaining self-awareness about her condition

jokes.text: Oh, I've only got a hundred dollars. I better bid shrewdly. First up is this video camera. One hundred dollars! $100 and 50 cents! Sold! Damn it!
jokes.setup: Homer plans to bid shrewdly with limited money
jokes.punchline: Immediately blows his entire budget on the first item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer Simpson behavior - planning wisdom followed by immediate impulsive failure

jokes.text: Check it out, Fat-wad! I'm better than you, and I'm only ten. Fat-wad! I love it!
jokes.setup: Bart insults Homer at carnival game
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely enjoys being called 'Fat-wad'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's childlike appreciation for creative insults

jokes.text: I'm number one! I beat my son! Victory is mine, so kiss my behind!
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating winning a carnival game
jokes.punchline: Rhyming victory chant that celebrates beating a child
jokes.explanation: Homer's poor sportsmanship and crude rhyming creates mild comedy

jokes.text: My son flips frogs like a girl! Yeah! A-boo, a-bah, a-who's your daddy?
jokes.setup: Homer continues celebrating
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical victory sounds
jokes.explanation: Extends the previous joke with diminishing returns - the nonsense sounds are less funny

jokes.text: Homer, do fries come with that shake? Sure do.
jokes.setup: Homer doing embarrassing victory dance
jokes.punchline: Marge treats his butt-shaking as a restaurant order
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual pun - Marge's deadpan delivery turning Homer's dance into a food order is perfectly crafted

jokes.text: He makes me look cool. And cool I am not.
jokes.setup: Ned observing Homer's embarrassing behavior
jokes.punchline: Ned's self-aware admission of uncoolness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders line - his earnest self-awareness about his lack of coolness

jokes.text: Boxer shot by wife. Beauty pageant diarrhea. Here's a new one: Big-ass Baryshnikov.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy browsing viral videos
jokes.punchline: Absurd video titles escalating to Homer as 'Big-ass Baryshnikov'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd internet content with the Baryshnikov comparison being particularly clever

jokes.text: Look at the American computer monkey! Dance-a, monkey, dance! Basta, basta. Take-a my picture with the Internet marmaluke-a!
jokes.setup: Homer's video has gone viral internationally
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated Italian stereotype mocking Homer
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotypical Italian accent and behavior - lazy comedy that hasn't aged well

jokes.text: The Internet wasn't created for mockery. It was supposed to help researchers at different universities share data sets. It was.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains the Internet's original purpose
jokes.punchline: Marge's simple agreement highlighting how far it's strayed
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about internet culture with Lisa's earnestness contrasted by Marge's resigned acceptance

jokes.text: I played the gentle giant, 'Stay-off-drugs-ia.'
jokes.setup: Football player mentions Disney Channel movie
jokes.punchline: Character name that's literally an anti-drug message
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that parodies Disney's heavy-handed educational content

jokes.text: 'L' is for 'loser' Which describes you, sir Don't try to stop me Just enjoy the view, sir. 'The view, sir.' Then show ass. Got it.
jokes.setup: Homer creating touchdown celebration choreography
jokes.punchline: Crude literalism of 'show ass' instruction
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny but relies on crude humor - the rhyme structure is decent but the payoff is cheap

jokes.text: Don't forget showboating. That's the cornerstone of my 'Pyramid of Success.'
jokes.setup: Homer teaching bad sportsmanship
jokes.punchline: Treating showboating as foundational life skill
jokes.explanation: Good parody of self-help culture with Homer positioning terrible behavior as wisdom

jokes.text: Grandpa, will you take me fishin'? Sorry, Jimmy, your Grandma and I are going to go have old-people sex.
jokes.setup: Child asks grandfather for innocent activity
jokes.punchline: TMI response about elderly sexuality
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that works due to the contrast between innocent request and inappropriate response

jokes.text: Daddy, if Cain and Abel were Adam and Eve's only children, how did they make more babies? Did they make babies with their mother? Or with each other?
jokes.setup: Child asking about Biblical story
jokes.punchline: Innocent questions exposing uncomfortable Biblical implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversive humor - child's innocent logic exposes the problematic implications of literal Biblical interpretation

jokes.text: Your mouth is hopin' for a soapin', boy. Now stop asking silly questions and go kill your brother!
jokes.setup: Ned threatened by theological questions
jokes.punchline: Redirects to actual murder while calling questions silly
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Ned considers theological questions inappropriate but casual murder acceptable for his film

jokes.text: You're going down, you potato-eatin' pansy!
jokes.setup: Homer choreographing anti-Irish celebration
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific stereotype insult
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotyping for humor - not particularly clever or well-crafted

jokes.text: I happen to know that the person inside that leprechaun suit is a single mother.
jokes.setup: Homer planning to attack mascot
jokes.punchline: Ned's oddly specific knowledge about mascot performer
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Ned's compassionate nature expressed through weirdly specific mascot knowledge

jokes.text: Deion, this is Timmy Thomas. He has Timmy Thomas disease.
jokes.setup: Homer introducing sick child
jokes.punchline: Disease named after the patient himself
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny concept that a disease would be named after its victim rather than discoverer

jokes.text: Lenny, can I move you from Wednesday at noon to Sunday at 6:00? Homer, you know that's when I play with neighborhood dogs.
jokes.setup: Homer scheduling dance lessons
jokes.punchline: Lenny's oddly specific social commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Simpsons absurdism - Lenny's completely serious commitment to playing with dogs

jokes.text: What the... Oh, I get it.
jokes.setup: Someone thanks Lovejoy for 'He-without-cineplex'
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's delayed recognition of the pun
jokes.explanation: Mild pun humor with Lovejoy's confused then understanding reaction

jokes.text: It turned me from an atheist to a hurray-theist!
jokes.setup: Frink praising Ned's Biblical film
jokes.punchline: Atheist/hurray-theist pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Frink's scientific character embracing religion

jokes.text: I would pay to see it again and again and again and again, but not six times.
jokes.setup: Frink expressing enthusiasm for the film
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary limit on his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in the arbitrary specificity of Frink's enthusiasm limit

jokes.text: Here's some guy's wallet. I am that guy!
jokes.setup: Someone offers found wallet as payment
jokes.punchline: Immediate identification by owner
jokes.explanation: Simple irony with good timing - wallet owner immediately present

jokes.text: And I've been looking for a way to launder the money I made selling club soda as flu vaccine.
jokes.setup: Burns discussing film investment
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of medical fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - casually mentioning elaborate criminal scheme

jokes.text: He left out all the good times Cain and Abel had when they were growing up.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on the violent Biblical film
jokes.punchline: Expecting happy family moments in a story that ends with murder
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Marge's attempt to find positive family moments even in dark stories

jokes.text: And sexy! King David stole someone else's wife, Mary Magdalene was a hooker...
jokes.setup: Discussion of Biblical violence
jokes.punchline: Bart highlighting Biblical sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Biblical content from Bart's irreverent perspective

jokes.text: I didn't know you could talk!
jokes.setup: Homer meeting Michelle Kwan
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks figure skaters can't speak
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity - treating figure skater like a trained animal

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but I do not understand English. Wait a second. I've read that you speak excellent English. Shut up, kid. I got a good thing going here.
jokes.setup: LeBron pretends not to speak English
jokes.punchline: Lisa calls him out, he drops the act
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about athlete interview clichés and media manipulation

jokes.text: Oh, I already do, if by 'razzle' you mean 'piety' and by 'dazzle' you mean 'Scriptural accuracy.'
jokes.setup: Homer suggests Lovejoy add razzle-dazzle to services
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy redefines entertainment as religious virtue
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Lovejoy's stuffy character

jokes.text: What a tool.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy's pretentious response
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Homer's blunt assessment of Lovejoy's pompousness

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I killed a baby. I'm a monster!
jokes.setup: Biblical film showing Solomon's judgment
jokes.punchline: Actor's delayed realization about his character
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - actor suddenly realizing the implications of Biblical story

jokes.text: I guess you'd rather see a film about a liberal European wizard school or the latest sexcapade of Miss Ashley Judd.
jokes.setup: Ned defending his violent Biblical films
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of secular entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ned's religious bias against popular culture

jokes.text: I guess you'd rather see a film about a liberal European wizard school or the latest sexcapade of Miss Ashley Judd.
jokes.setup: Ned defending his violent Biblical films
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reference to Harry Potter and Ashley Judd movies as competing entertainment
jokes.explanation: Captures Ned's cultural conservatism and specific 2005-era references

jokes.text: Solar. Hydroelectric. A mix of conservation and wind. Who told you about those? A talking tree in a commercial.
jokes.setup: Burns asks about alternative energy sources
jokes.punchline: Environmental knowledge from advertising
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how people get environmental information from corporate marketing

jokes.text: Tonight, the Clipper and the Stripper, a Jones that's Chipper, and did Joe Torre shoot Flipper?
jokes.setup: Sports show intro with rhyming headlines
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd rhyming news items
jokes.explanation: Parody of sports show gimmicky headlines with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: This is either about me or steroids.
jokes.setup: Sports show criticizing professional sports problems
jokes.punchline: Homer's limited perspective on sports controversies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's self-awareness about his impact on sports

jokes.text: I'm the worst thing to happen to sports since Fox.
jokes.setup: Homer feeling guilty about his influence
jokes.punchline: Meta-reference to Fox Sports/Fox Broadcasting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where Fox's The Simpsons mocks Fox Sports - perfect self-aware humor

jokes.text: Maybe he can tell me how to get this off. It soaked through to the other side.
jokes.setup: Homer has something stuck to him he wants removed
jokes.punchline: Whatever it is has penetrated completely through his body
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer physical comedy. The 'soaked through' detail adds absurdity

jokes.text: Plus, we're making millions in player fines, which I'm going to use to bankroll a musical about The J. Geils Band!
jokes.setup: Commissioner explaining financial benefits
jokes.punchline: Bizarre investment in J. Geils Band musical
jokes.explanation: Random absurdity that works due to its complete unexpectedness

jokes.text: You suckers! I would've done it for free! Fine, do it for free. Damn it! Well, I'll still do it. Suckers. I would've paid you. Fine, pay us! Oh, damn it! Will you take a check? No! Damn it!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about Super Bowl opportunity
jokes.punchline: Series of negotiation self-defeats
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer Simpson negotiation - enthusiastically undermining himself repeatedly

jokes.text: You suckers! I would've done it for free! Fine, do it for free. Damn it! Well, I'll still do it. Suckers. I would've paid you. Fine, pay us! Oh, damn it! Will you take a check? No! Damn it!
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally revealing he'd work for free
jokes.punchline: Escalating negotiation that keeps getting worse for Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating comedy showing Homer's inability to negotiate, perfectly structured

jokes.text: ...to the marriage of PacMan and Ms. PacMan in Super Bowl XVI...
jokes.setup: Documentary about Super Bowl halftime show history
jokes.punchline: Fictional video game character wedding
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about fake halftime show history

jokes.text: Oh, every single Super Bowl halftime show has been great
jokes.setup: Homer watching halftime show montage
jokes.punchline: Obvious sarcasm about notoriously bad halftime shows
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm about halftime show quality - not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm like Michael Moore, except I'm skinny, my jeans are washed, and God loves me.
jokes.setup: Ned comparing himself to documentary filmmaker
jokes.punchline: Series of self-righteous contrasts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders line - self-righteous comparison that reveals his judgmental nature

jokes.text: Have you tried checking the oil filter?
jokes.setup: Ned seeking advice about distribution problems
jokes.punchline: Homer offers mechanical advice to media problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's mechanical mindset applied inappropriately

jokes.text: Yeah. You help me, and I, in turn, am helped by you.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to describe mutual benefit
jokes.punchline: Redundant phrasing that says nothing
jokes.explanation: Homer's circular logic that sounds profound but means nothing

jokes.text: and it's scheduled for demolition early next month. America's priorities are a joke.
jokes.setup: New $300 million stadium completed one week ago
jokes.punchline: Immediate demolition planned
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of wasteful sports spending and American priorities

jokes.text: But don't stop watching. In the second half, points count double.
jokes.setup: Boring blowout game at halftime
jokes.punchline: Absurd rule change to maintain viewer interest
jokes.explanation: Absurd sports rule that parodies TV's desperate attempts to maintain viewership

jokes.text: How could you afford this? I hustled a lot of pool. Uh, hey, you want to play? I got to warn you, I ain't that good.
jokes.setup: Question about Moe affording Super Bowl sponsorship
jokes.punchline: Moe immediately trying to hustle the questioner
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - can't help himself from trying to hustle

jokes.text: I left the People's Republic for this?
jokes.setup: Michelle Kwan watching Homer's biblical halftime show
jokes.punchline: Suggesting communist China was better than this
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash humor with Kwan's political reference

jokes.text: You try to raise your kids as secular humanists, but these showbiz types keep shoving religion down our throats!
jokes.setup: Outrage about religious content in halftime show
jokes.punchline: Complaint about religious indoctrination
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of typical complaints about secular media content

jokes.text: Omelets for dinner? This is the best day of my life. Really? Didn't you just sign a $90 million contract? That was a good day, too.
jokes.setup: Homer serves omelets made from eggs thrown at his house
jokes.punchline: Deion's simple appreciation rivaling his massive contract signing
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing simple pleasures rivaling wealth

jokes.text: I should've just sent a crocodile into space like I originally planned.
jokes.setup: Homer regretting his halftime show choice
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd alternative plan
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Homer logic - space crocodile as reasonable entertainment option

jokes.text: Omelets for dinner? This is the best day of my life. Really? Didn't you just sign a $90 million contract? That was a good day, too.
jokes.setup: LeBron excited about dinner omelets
jokes.punchline: Puts omelets above $90 million contract
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between simple pleasures and material success
